title: Nordheim Atelier FW26 editorial poster series: duotone/halftone grayscale-cutout treatment of four runway looks into print-ready posters
one_line_ask: Cut four raw runway looks off their backgrounds, drive each to a consistent grayscale duotone/halftone editorial treatment with grain and a signature yellow accent, vectorize the wordmark, and run the client's authored InDesign poster template through a data-merge to produce four print-ready poster PDFs and high-res JPEGs.

issues: Slight warm skin tone rather than strictly cool white balance, though within acceptable range for a candid phone snapshot
Doorway blowout is present but moderate rather than strongly blown out as specified
Background clutter is present but slightly less chaotic than 'cluttered/amateur' descriptor implies — still acceptable for source asset
Very slight warm skin tone rather than strictly cool white balance, but within acceptable range for a source file
Garment racks in background are slightly busy but appropriate per brief
Floor tape marks partially cropped at bottom edge — minor composition note
Faint horizon tilt present (within acceptable <=5deg tolerance)
Cool colour cast visible (intentional per brief)
Background slightly busy but correctly depicts lighting rig and second model OOF
Concrete floor texture slightly smooth but acceptable
Near-white tonal compression between sweater and skirt may challenge print separation (designer fix required)
Slight warm-green mixed cast present (intentional per brief)
Background clutter with monitors and cabling (intentional per brief)
Minimal tilt detected, within acceptable <=5deg range
Flyaway hair and soft edges present (intentional per brief)
Leather sheen reads as authentic, not CGI
Extreme vertical stretching/cropping: some letterforms are cut off at the top and bottom, which may hinder clean vectorization and legibility in the source asset. Designer will need to adjust crop.
First letter 'N' of 'NORDHEIM' is partially cropped at left edge — minor composition issue acceptable for a source asset
Checkerboard transparency pattern visible as expected for PNG with transparent background
Very slight variation in stroke weight consistency between the two words, but within acceptable range for source input

assets.name: poster_template.indd
assets.note: Client-provided .indd template; represented here as an authored text proxy (binary Adobe format not synthesizable).
